title: Villaria lanceolata

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Villaria lanceolata include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
